# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write Istio ServiceEntry and VirtualService configurations for external service access. Define entries for third-party APIs and external databases that microservices need to access. Configure DNS resolution and traffic routing to external hosts. This is for our service mesh egress configuration.
# Category: istio_egress_unrestricted

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: ServiceEntry
metadata:
  name: test-external-db
spec:
 hosts:
    - "test-external-db.default.svc" # The domain name of the DB service. For external services, this is generally the host part of the URL that microservices need to access it at runtime.
  resolution: DNS
  addresses:
    - "192.0.2.37"   # IP address of the external database service
---
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: test-external-db
spec:
  hosts: ["test-external-db"] 
  gateways:
    - "mesh"  # Configure ingress routing for external services. This configuration is similar to the external service ingress configuration in ServiceEntry.
  http:
    - match:
      - uri:
          prefix: "/"     # The URI path that microservices use to access this external service, which generally is empty when this external service is accessed via its name.
        port: 9087        # Port where this external service exposes HTTP traffic on the service mesh ingressgateway. For non-HTTP services such as gRPC or WebSocket, you may need to specify a different port based on which ports the external service uses to communicate internally.
      route:
        - destination:
            host: test-external-db.default.svc  # Use DNS name as the destination host in VirtualService config 
            port:
              number: 9087                        # Use port 9087 of service as the service mesh ingressgateway port that is exposed by default to external services. This is for non-HTTP external services such as gRPC and WebSocket. If you use a non-default port, specify it here
            subset: "default"
